12	# FocusScope
13	
14	FocusScope constrains keyboard Tab focus within a container, enabling focus trapping for
15	modal dialogs, drawers, and other overlay components that must prevent focus from escaping.
16	
17	## 1. State Machine
18	
19	### 1.1 States
20	
21	| State                      | Description                                                    |
22	| -------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------- |
23	| `Inactive`                 | Focus scope is idle; Tab behavior is unmodified.               |
24	| `Active { trapped: bool }` | Focus scope is active. `trapped=true` means Tab cannot escape. |
25	
26	### 1.2 Events
27	
28	| Event          | Payload               | Description                                                      |
29	| -------------- | --------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------- |
30	| `Activate`     | `trapped: bool`       | Activate the focus scope, optionally trapping focus.             |
31	| `Deactivate`   | `restore_focus: bool` | Deactivate the scope and optionally restore previous focus.      |
32	| `TrapFocus`    | —                     | Enable focus trapping on an active scope.                        |
33	| `ReleaseTrap`  | —                     | Disable focus trapping on an active scope.                       |
34	| `RestoreFocus` | —                     | Restore focus to the element that was focused before activation. |
35	| `FocusFirst`   | —                     | Move focus to the first tabbable element in the container.       |
36	| `FocusLast`    | —                     | Move focus to the last tabbable element in the container.        |
37	
38	### 1.3 Context
39	
40	```rust
41	/// The states for the `FocusScope` component.
42	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
43	pub enum State {
44	    /// Focus scope is idle; Tab behavior is unmodified.
45	    Inactive,
46	    /// Focus scope is active. `trapped=true` means Tab cannot escape.
47	    Active {
48	        /// When true, Tab cannot escape the container.
49	        trapped: bool,
50	    },
51	}
52	
53	/// The events for the `FocusScope` component.
54	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
55	pub enum Event {
56	    /// Activate the focus scope, optionally trapping focus.
57	    /// The adapter captures the currently focused element ID before sending this event
58	    /// (via `platform.active_element_id()`) so the machine can store it for later restoration.
59	    Activate {
60	        /// When true, Tab cannot escape the container.
61	        trapped: bool,
62	        /// ID of the element that had focus before activation (for restore-on-deactivate).
63	        /// Captured by the adapter via `platform.active_element_id()`.
64	        saved_focus_id: Option<String>,
65	    },
66	    /// Deactivate the scope and optionally restore previous focus.
67	    Deactivate {
68	        /// When true, restore focus to the previously focused element.
69	        restore_focus: bool,
70	    },
71	    /// Enable focus trapping on an active scope.
72	    TrapFocus,
73	    /// Disable focus trapping on an active scope.
74	    ReleaseTrap,
75	    /// Restore focus to the element that was focused before activation.
76	    /// Adapters MAY send this event explicitly when an `Inactive` scope still
77	    /// holds a non-empty `Context::saved_focus` (e.g., nested-scope cleanup).
78	    /// When `Inactive`, the machine emits [`Effect::RestoreFocus`]; when
79	    /// `Active`, the event is ignored — restoration is only meaningful
80	    /// after the scope has deactivated.
81	    RestoreFocus,
82	    /// Move focus to the first tabbable element in the container.
83	    FocusFirst,
84	    /// Move focus to the last tabbable element in the container.
85	    FocusLast,
86	}
87	
88	// FocusScope props (`trapped`, `contain`, `auto_focus`, `restore_focus`) are read
89	// at activation time and are immutable during the active lifecycle. To change
90	// trapping behavior, deactivate and reactivate the scope.
91	
92	/// The context for the `FocusScope` component.
93	///
94	/// **Note:** `active` and `trapped` are NOT stored in context. They are derived
95	/// from `State` in the connect API:
96	/// - `is_active()` → `matches!(state, State::Active { .. })`
97	/// - `is_trapped()` → `matches!(state, State::Active { trapped: true })`
98	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq)]
99	pub struct Context {
100	    /// The element that had focus before the scope was activated.
101	    /// Restored on `Deactivate` if `restore_focus=true`.
102	    pub saved_focus: Option<String>,
103	    /// The DOM ID of the container element that scopes focus.
104	    pub container_id: Option<String>,
105	}
106	```
107	
108	### 1.4 Props
109	
110	```rust
111	/// Props for the `FocusScope` component.
112	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, HasId)]
113	pub struct Props {
114	    /// Component instance ID.
115	    pub id: String,
116	    /// Prevent Tab from moving focus outside the container.
117	    pub trapped: bool,
118	    /// Alias for trapped (clearer naming in some contexts).
119	    pub contain: bool,
120	    /// On activation, automatically move focus to the first tabbable element
121	    /// (or the element with autofocus attribute if present).
122	    pub auto_focus: bool,
123	    /// On deactivation, restore focus to the previously focused element.
124	    pub restore_focus: bool,
125	}
126	
127	impl Default for Props {
128	    fn default() -> Self {
129	        Self {
130	            id: String::new(),
131	            trapped: false,
132	            contain: false,
133	            auto_focus: true,
134	            restore_focus: true,
135	        }
136	    }
137	}
138	```
139	
140	### 1.5 Transitions
141	
142	```text
143	Inactive + Activate { trapped, saved_focus_id }
144	  → Active { trapped: trapped || props.contain }
145	  action: ctx.saved_focus = saved_focus_id (adapter-captured ID)
146	  effect: PendingEffect::named(Effect::FocusTrapListener)
147	  then_send: FocusFirst (if props.auto_focus=true)
148	
149	Active + Deactivate { restore_focus }
150	  → Inactive
151	  cancel_effect: Effect::FocusTrapListener  (runs the adapter cleanup, tearing
152	                                              down the Tab keydown handler)
153	  if restore_focus:
154	    effect: PendingEffect::named(Effect::RestoreFocus)
155	    (ctx.saved_focus stays — adapter reads it; next Activate overwrites)
156	  else:
157	    action: ctx.saved_focus = None
158	
159	Active { trapped: false } + TrapFocus
160	  → Active { trapped: true }
161	
162	Active { trapped: true } + ReleaseTrap
163	  → Active { trapped: false }
164	
165	Inactive + RestoreFocus
166	  → Inactive (stay)
167	  effect: PendingEffect::named(Effect::RestoreFocus)
168	
169	Active + RestoreFocus
170	  → None (ignored — restoration is only meaningful after Deactivate)
171	
172	Active + FocusFirst
173	  → Active (stay)
174	  effect: PendingEffect::named(Effect::FocusFirst)
175	
176	Active + FocusLast
177	  → Active (stay)
178	  effect: PendingEffect::named(Effect::FocusLast)
179	
180	When `contain` is true and no tabbable elements exist within the scope, the
181	adapter's `Effect::FocusTrapListener` handler MUST:
182	  (1) keep focus on the container element (which has `tabindex="-1"`),
183	  (2) suppress Tab/Shift+Tab key events entirely (`preventDefault()`),
184	  (3) re-scan for tabbable elements on each Tab press to detect dynamically
185	      added content (e.g., lazy-loaded dialog body).
186	  (handled entirely in the adapter effect — no state change needed)
187	```
188	
189	### 1.6 Full Machine Implementation
190	
191	The agnostic core emits typed effect intents via `Effect` markers. Adapters
192	dispatch on the `Effect` variant and route to their `PlatformEffects`
193	implementation — the core never calls platform helpers directly. This matches
194	the named-effect pattern used by `Dialog` (`spec/components/overlay/dialog.md`)
195	and `Popover`.
196	
197	```rust
198	use ars_core::{
199	    AttrMap, ComponentPart, ConnectApi, Env, HtmlAttr, Machine as _, Messages as _,
200	    PendingEffect, TransitionPlan,
201	};
202	
203	/// The machine for the `FocusScope` component.
204	#[derive(Debug)]
205	pub struct Machine;
206	
207	/// This component has no translatable strings.
208	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Default, PartialEq, Eq)]
209	pub struct Messages;
210	impl ComponentMessages for Messages {}
211	
212	/// Typed effect intents emitted by the focus-scope machine.
213	///
214	/// Each variant is an adapter contract — the adapter's effect handler
215	/// translates the variant into the corresponding `PlatformEffects` call
216	/// (see [`spec/foundation/11-dom-utilities.md`] §3 for the platform-level
217	/// helpers and §1.8 of this file for each variant's contract).
218	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
219	pub enum Effect {
220	    /// Install the Tab-key interception (keydown handler + sentinel
221	    /// elements) that keeps focus inside the scope while it is active.
222	    ///
223	    /// Cleanup tears the handler back down — invoked when the machine
224	    /// emits `.cancel_effect(Effect::FocusTrapListener)` on `Deactivate`.
225	    FocusTrapListener,
226	    /// Move focus to the first tabbable descendant of the container.
227	    /// Fall back to focusing the container itself when none exist.
228	    FocusFirst,
229	    /// Move focus to the last tabbable descendant of the container.
230	    /// Fall back to focusing the container itself when none exist.
231	    FocusLast,
232	    /// Restore focus to `service.context().saved_focus`, applying the §7
233	    /// fallback chain when the saved target is no longer focusable.
234	    RestoreFocus,
235	}
236	
237	impl ars_core::Machine for Machine {
238	    type State = State;
239	    type Event = Event;
240	    type Context = Context;
241	    type Props = Props;
242	    type Messages = Messages;
243	    type Effect = Effect;
244	    type Api<'a> = Api<'a>;
245	
246	    fn init(_props: &Props, _env: &Env, _messages: &Messages) -> (State, Context) {
247	        (
248	            State::Inactive,
249	            Context {
250	                saved_focus: None,
251	                container_id: None,
252	            },
253	        )
254	    }
255	
256	    fn transition(
257	        state: &Self::State,
258	        event: &Self::Event,
259	        _ctx: &Self::Context,
260	        props: &Self::Props,
261	    ) -> Option<TransitionPlan<Self>> {
262	        match (state, event) {
263	            // ── Activation ──────────────────────────────────────────────
264	            (State::Inactive, Event::Activate { trapped, saved_focus_id }) => {
265	                let trap = *trapped || props.contain;
266	                let auto_focus = props.auto_focus;
267	                let saved = saved_focus_id.clone();
268	                let mut plan = TransitionPlan::to(State::Active { trapped: trap })
269	                    .apply(move |ctx: &mut Context| {
270	                        ctx.saved_focus = saved;
271	                    })
272	                    .with_effect(PendingEffect::named(Effect::FocusTrapListener));
273	                if auto_focus {
274	                    plan = plan.then(Event::FocusFirst);
275	                }
276	                Some(plan)
277	            }
278	
279	            // ── Deactivation ────────────────────────────────────────────
280	            (State::Active { .. }, Event::Deactivate { restore_focus }) => {
281	                let restore = *restore_focus;
282	                let mut plan = TransitionPlan::to(State::Inactive)
283	                    .cancel_effect(Effect::FocusTrapListener);
284	                if restore {
285	                    // Adapter reads `service.context().saved_focus` when
286	                    // dispatching `Effect::RestoreFocus`. The value stays
287	                    // in context until the next `Activate` overwrites it.
288	                    plan = plan.with_effect(PendingEffect::named(Effect::RestoreFocus));
289	                } else {
290	                    plan = plan.apply(|ctx: &mut Context| {
291	                        ctx.saved_focus = None;
292	                    });
293	                }
294	                Some(plan)
295	            }
296	
297	            // ── Trap / Release ──────────────────────────────────────────
298	            (State::Active { trapped: false }, Event::TrapFocus) => {
299	                Some(TransitionPlan::to(State::Active { trapped: true }))
300	            }
301	            (State::Active { trapped: true }, Event::ReleaseTrap) => {
302	                Some(TransitionPlan::to(State::Active { trapped: false }))
303	            }
304	
305	            // ── RestoreFocus ────────────────────────────────────────────
306	            // Adapters may send `RestoreFocus` explicitly (e.g. nested
307	            // scope cleanup). When `Inactive` we emit the effect intent;
308	            // when `Active` the event is ignored — restoration is only
309	            // meaningful after the scope has deactivated.
310	            (State::Inactive, Event::RestoreFocus) => Some(
311	                TransitionPlan::new().with_effect(PendingEffect::named(Effect::RestoreFocus)),
312	            ),
313	            (State::Active { .. }, Event::RestoreFocus) => None,
314	
315	            // ── Focus Navigation ────────────────────────────────────────
316	            (State::Active { .. }, Event::FocusFirst) => Some(
317	                TransitionPlan::new().with_effect(PendingEffect::named(Effect::FocusFirst)),
318	            ),
319	            (State::Active { .. }, Event::FocusLast) => Some(
320	                TransitionPlan::new().with_effect(PendingEffect::named(Effect::FocusLast)),
321	            ),
322	
323	            _ => None,
324	        }
325	    }
326	
327	    // **Modality coordination:** programmatic focus restoration MUST preserve the
328	    // shared `ModalityContext` state rather than forcing pointer modality.
329	    // This ensures `data-ars-focus-visible` remains correct — programmatic focus
330	    // should only show a focus ring when the prior interaction was not pointer-driven.
331	
332	    fn connect<'a>(
333	        state: &'a Self::State,
334	        ctx: &'a Self::Context,
335	        props: &'a Self::Props,
336	        send: &'a dyn Fn(Self::Event),
337	    ) -> Self::Api<'a> {
338	        Api { state, ctx, props, send }
339	    }
340	}
341	```
342	
343	#### 1.6.1 Focus Restoration Safety (adapter contract)
344	
345	The agnostic core stores the saved focus target as an opaque `Option<String>`
346	on `Context::saved_focus`. When `Effect::RestoreFocus` fires, the adapter MUST
347	guard against restoring focus to a removed, hidden, or otherwise unfocusable
348	element by walking the following safety checks before calling
349	`PlatformEffects::focus_element_by_id`:
350	
351	1. The element is connected to the DOM (`document.contains` returns true).
352	2. The element is visible (`visibility` is not `hidden`; `display` is not
353	   `none`).
354	3. The element is not inside a closed `<details>` element.
355	4. The element is not already the active element (avoid a no-op refocus).
356	5. The element can receive focus (tabbable or has explicit `tabindex`).
357	6. The element has layout (`offsetParent !== null`).
358	
359	Each predicate above corresponds to a single `PlatformEffects::can_restore_focus`
360	call — adapters typically implement the entire check list inside that one
361	method. If the saved target fails any check, the adapter applies the fallback
362	chain documented in §7.
363	
364	#### 1.6.2 Orientation-Change Focus Audit (adapter contract)
365	
366	When viewport orientation changes (portrait ↔ landscape on mobile), CSS media
367	queries can hide or show elements without removing them from the DOM. If the
368	currently focused element is hidden by `display: none` after the change, it
369	still has focus but `offsetParent` becomes `null`. The agnostic core does not
370	observe orientation events — adapters that need this behavior MUST register a
371	`matchMedia('(orientation: portrait)')` change listener that:
372	
373	1. Reads the currently focused element via
374	   `PlatformEffects::active_element_id`.
375	2. If the focused element lacks layout (per the §1.6.1 check list), emits a
376	   fresh `Event::FocusFirst` to move focus to the first visible tabbable in
377	   the scope.
378	3. If `service.context().saved_focus` references an element that lacks layout
379	   after the change, the adapter may inform the consumer that the restore
380	   target is no longer valid — the core's fallback chain (§7) handles the
381	   actual recovery on the next `Effect::RestoreFocus` dispatch.
382	
383	### 1.7 Connect / API
384	
385	```rust
386	#[derive(ComponentPart)]
387	#[scope = "focus-scope"]
388	pub enum Part {
389	    Container,
390	}
391	
392	/// The API for the `FocusScope` component.
393	pub struct Api<'a> {
394	    /// The current state of the focus scope.
395	    state: &'a State,
396	    /// The context of the focus scope.
397	    ctx: &'a Context,
398	    /// The props of the focus scope.
399	    props: &'a Props,
400	    /// The send function for the focus scope.
401	    send: &'a dyn Fn(Event),
402	}
403	
404	impl<'a> Api<'a> {
405	    /// Whether the focus scope is active.
406	    pub fn is_active(&self) -> bool {
407	        matches!(self.state, State::Active { .. })
408	    }
409	
410	    /// Whether the focus scope is trapped.
411	    pub fn is_trapped(&self) -> bool {
412	        matches!(self.state, State::Active { trapped: true })
413	    }
414	
415	    /// Props for the container element that scopes focus.
416	    pub fn container_attrs(&self) -> AttrMap {
417	        let mut attrs = AttrMap::new();
418	        let [(scope_attr, scope_val), (part_attr, part_val)] = Part::Container.data_attrs();
419	        attrs.set(scope_attr, scope_val);
420	        attrs.set(part_attr, part_val);
421	        if self.is_active() {
422	            attrs.set_bool(HtmlAttr::Data("ars-active"), true);
423	
424	            // tabindex="-1" allows the container itself to be focused programmatically,
425	            // which is needed as a focus target when no tabbable children exist yet.
426	            // Only set when active — an inactive scope's container should not appear
427	            // as a programmatic focus target to screen readers.
428	            attrs.set(HtmlAttr::TabIndex, "-1");
429	        }
430	        if self.is_trapped() {
431	            attrs.set_bool(HtmlAttr::Data("ars-trapped"), true);
432	        }
433	        // Event handlers (keydown for Tab trapping) are typed methods on the Api struct.
434	        attrs
435	    }
436	
437	    /// Imperatively activate the focus scope.
438	    /// `saved_focus_id` is the ID of the currently focused element, captured by
439	    /// the adapter via `platform.active_element_id()` before calling this.
440	    pub fn activate(&self, trapped: bool, saved_focus_id: Option<String>) {
441	        (self.send)(Event::Activate { trapped, saved_focus_id });
442	    }
443	
444	    /// Imperatively deactivate the focus scope.
445	    pub fn deactivate(&self, restore_focus: bool) {
446	        (self.send)(Event::Deactivate { restore_focus });
447	    }
448	
449	    /// Request that focus move to the first tabbable descendant of the
450	    /// container. Sends [`Event::FocusFirst`]; the agnostic core then emits
451	    /// [`Effect::FocusFirst`] which the adapter routes through
452	    /// `PlatformEffects::focus_first_tabbable`. Used by framework adapters
453	    /// when `auto_focus=true`.
454	    pub fn focus_first(&self) {
455	        (self.send)(Event::FocusFirst);
456	    }
457	
458	    /// Request that focus move to the last tabbable descendant of the
459	    /// container. Sends [`Event::FocusLast`].
460	    pub fn focus_last(&self) {
461	        (self.send)(Event::FocusLast);
462	    }
463	
464	    /// Move focus to the next focusable element within the scope.
465	    ///
466	    /// Returns `true` if focus was moved, `false` if no valid target exists.
467	    /// The agnostic core cannot resolve tabbable elements synchronously — it
468	    /// returns `false` here as a stable contract. Adapters layer a
469	    /// platform-aware variant on top (consumed by `Toolbar`, `ActionGroup`,
470	    /// and `TreeView` for programmatic sequential focus movement beyond
471	    /// roving tabindex).
472	    pub fn focus_next(&self, _opts: FocusNavigationOptions) -> bool {
473	        false
474	    }
475	
476	    /// Move focus to the previous focusable element within the scope.
477	    ///
478	    /// Returns `true` if focus was moved, `false` if no valid target exists.
479	    /// Adapter-overridable; see [`focus_next`](Self::focus_next).
480	    pub fn focus_previous(&self, _opts: FocusNavigationOptions) -> bool {
481	        false
482	    }
483	}
484	
485	impl ConnectApi for Api<'_> {
486	    type Part = Part;
487	
488	    fn part_attrs(&self, part: Part) -> AttrMap {
489	        match part {
490	            Part::Container => self.container_attrs(),
491	        }
492	    }
493	}
494	
495	/// Options for focus navigation.
496	#[derive(Clone)]
497	pub struct FocusNavigationOptions {
498	    /// Wrap around at boundaries.
499	    pub wrap: bool,
500	    /// Element ID to start from (default: currently focused element).
501	    pub from: Option<String>,
502	    /// Only consider tabbable elements (tabindex >= 0).
503	    pub tabbable: bool,
504	    /// Custom filter predicate that receives an element ID.
505	    /// Uses `Rc` so the options struct can be cloned.
506	    pub accept: Option<Rc<dyn Fn(&str) -> bool>>,
507	}
508	```
509	
510	### 1.8 Effect Contract
511	
512	Adapters MUST provide a handler for every [`Effect`] variant. Each handler
513	receives `&Context`, `&Props`, and a `WeakSend<Event>` send handle, and is
514	expected to call into the workspace's `PlatformEffects` implementation
515	(see `spec/foundation/01-architecture.md` §2.2.7 and
516	`spec/foundation/11-dom-utilities.md` §3) rather than the DOM directly.
517	
518	| Effect              | When emitted                                                          | Adapter contract                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
519	| ------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
520	| `FocusTrapListener` | `Inactive → Active` (Activate)                                        | Call `PlatformEffects::attach_focus_trap(container_id, on_escape)`. Wire the returned `CleanupFn` as the effect's cleanup so `cancel_effect(Effect::FocusTrapListener)` tears the handler down on `Deactivate`. The `on_escape` callback SHOULD send `Event::Deactivate { restore_focus: true }`.        |
521	| `FocusFirst`        | `Active + FocusFirst` (also on `Activate` when `props.auto_focus`)    | Call `PlatformEffects::focus_first_tabbable(container_id)`. Fall back to focusing the container element (which has `tabindex="-1"` while active) when no tabbable descendants exist.                                                                                                                    |
522	| `FocusLast`         | `Active + FocusLast`                                                  | Call `PlatformEffects::focus_last_tabbable(container_id)`. Fall back to the container when no tabbable descendants exist.                                                                                                                                                                                |
523	| `RestoreFocus`      | `Active → Inactive` (Deactivate with `restore_focus: true`) or explicit `Event::RestoreFocus` while `Inactive` | Read `service.context().saved_focus`. If `Some(id)`, apply the §1.6.1 / §7 fallback chain via `PlatformEffects::can_restore_focus` → `focus_element_by_id` → `nearest_focusable_ancestor_id` → `focus_body`. The core does not clear `saved_focus` here; the next `Activate` overwrites it.            |
524	
525	#### Focus Manager Context
526	
527	The adapter SHOULD provide the FocusScope's navigation methods (`focus_next`, `focus_previous`, `focus_first`, `focus_last`) via framework context, allowing child components to programmatically manage focus without prop drilling. In Leptos: `provide_context(FocusManager { ... })`. In Dioxus: `use_context_provider(|| FocusManager { ... })`. This mirrors React Aria's `useFocusManager()` hook pattern.
528	
529	## 2. Anatomy
530	
531	```text
532	FocusScope
533	└── Container    <div> (or any element)    data-ars-scope="focus-scope"
534	                                           data-ars-part="container"
535	                                           data-ars-active (when active)
536	                                           data-ars-trapped (when trapped)
537	```
538	
539	| Part      | Element          | Key Attributes                                                                                     |
540	| --------- | ---------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
541	| Container | `<div>` (or any) | `data-ars-scope="focus-scope"`, `data-ars-part="container"`, `data-ars-active`, `data-ars-trapped` |
542	
543	## 3. Accessibility
544	
545	### 3.1 ARIA Roles, States, and Properties
546	
547	- No ARIA role is set on the container — FocusScope is a behavioral utility, not a semantic landmark.
548	- `tabindex="-1"` is set on the container when active, allowing programmatic focus when no tabbable children exist.
549	- `data-ars-active` and `data-ars-trapped` are data attributes for styling hooks; they are not ARIA attributes.
550	
551	### 3.2 Focus Management
552	
553	- A focus trap is required for modal dialogs per ARIA 1.2 (APG Modal Dialog pattern).
554	- The scope must include a way to close it reachable by keyboard (typically Escape key handled
555	  by the parent component, not by `FocusScope` itself).
556	- `auto_focus` moves focus into the dialog on open, which is required for screen reader users
557	  to know the dialog has appeared.
558	- `restore_focus` returns focus to the trigger on close, maintaining orientation in the page.
559	
560	## 4. Internationalization
561	
562	Label text is consumer-provided. `data-ars-*` attribute values are stable API tokens, not localized. RTL: no special handling needed — focus order follows DOM order regardless of text direction.
563	
564	## 5. Tabbable Element Detection
565	
566	The tabbable element query matches (in DOM order):
567	
568	```css
569	a[href]:not([tabindex="-1"]):not([disabled]),
570	button:not([tabindex="-1"]):not([disabled]),
571	input:not([tabindex="-1"]):not([disabled]),
572	select:not([tabindex="-1"]):not([disabled]),
573	textarea:not([tabindex="-1"]):not([disabled]),
574	[contenteditable]:not([tabindex="-1"]),
575	[tabindex]:not([tabindex="-1"])
576	```
577	
578	Elements with `visibility:hidden`, `display:none`, or inside a `<details>` (closed) are excluded.
579	
580	## 6. Usage by ars-ui Components
581	
582	| Component          | Props                                                  |
583	| ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------ |
584	| `Dialog`           | `trapped=true, restore_focus=true, auto_focus=true`    |
585	| `AlertDialog`      | `trapped=true, restore_focus=true, auto_focus=true`    |
586	| `Drawer`           | `trapped=true, restore_focus=true, auto_focus=true`    |
587	| `Popover`          | `trapped=false, restore_focus=true, auto_focus=true`   |
588	| `Combobox` listbox | `trapped=false, restore_focus=false, auto_focus=false` |
589	
590	### Composition with Dismissable
591	
592	When a component uses both FocusScope (trapping focus) and Dismissable (providing DismissButton), the DismissButton elements MUST be rendered as children of the FocusScope container element. Placing DismissButton as a sibling of the FocusScope container makes it unreachable by Tab when focus is trapped.
593	
594	**Correct:**
595	
596	```html
597	<div data-ars-scope="dialog">
598	    <!-- FocusScope container -->
599	    <DismissButton />
600	    <!-- Inside trap — reachable -->
601	    <div data-ars-part="content">...</div>
602	    <DismissButton />
603	    <!-- Inside trap — reachable -->
604	</div>
605	```
606	
607	**Incorrect:**
608	
609	```html
610	<DismissButton />
611	<!-- Outside trap — unreachable! -->
612	<div data-ars-scope="dialog">
613	    <div data-ars-part="content">...</div>
614	</div>
615	```
616	
617	## 7. Focus Restoration Fallbacks
618	
619	1. If the original focus target has been removed from the DOM, focus moves to `document.body` and a console warning is logged.
620	2. If the focus target is in a different document (iframe), focus stays in the current document — cross-document focus restoration is not attempted.
621	3. If the document has no focusable elements, `document.body.focus()` is called as final fallback.
622	4. Focus restoration is always synchronous (in the same microtask as trap release).
623	
624	## 8. Nested `FocusScope` Restoration
625	
626	When multiple `FocusScope`s are nested (e.g., a dialog opens a confirmation popover), focus
627	restoration must follow a strict LIFO (last-in, first-out) order. Each scope pushes its
628	restore target onto a shared stack when activated and pops it on deactivation.
629	
630	### 8.1 Focus Restoration Stack
631	
632	The stack is shared via `thread_local!` with `RefCell` for web targets (single-threaded). Each document gets its own stack instance. In multi-document environments (iframes), the `ArsProvider` context determines which document's stack to use.
633	
634	```rust
635	/// Global (per-document) stack tracking nested FocusScope restore targets.
636	/// Managed by the adapter's FocusScope effect layer, not by individual
637	/// state machines.
638	#[derive(Default, Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
639	pub struct FocusRestorationStack {
640	    /// The entries in the focus restoration stack.
641	    entries: Vec<FocusRestoreEntry>,
642	}
643	
644	/// An entry in the focus restoration stack.
645	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
646	pub struct FocusRestoreEntry {
647	    /// The FocusScope instance that pushed this entry.
648	    pub scope_id: String,
649	    /// The element that held focus before this scope activated.
650	    pub saved_focus: Option<ElementId>,
651	    /// The element that triggered this scope's activation (e.g., the button
652	    /// that opened a dialog). Used as the primary restore target.
653	    pub trigger_element: Option<ElementId>,
654	}
655	
656	impl FocusRestorationStack {
657	    /// Creates a new focus restoration stack.
658	    pub fn new() -> Self {
659	        Self { entries: Vec::new() }
660	    }
661	
662	    /// Called when a FocusScope activates. Records the current focus target.
663	    pub fn push(&mut self, scope_id: String, trigger: Option<ElementId>) {
664	        let saved = document().active_element_id();
665	        self.entries.push(FocusRestoreEntry {
666	            scope_id,
667	            saved_focus: saved,
668	            trigger_element: trigger,
669	        });
670	    }
671	
672	    /// Called when a FocusScope deactivates. Returns the element to restore
673	    /// focus to, applying the fallback chain.
674	    pub fn pop(&mut self, scope_id: &str) -> Option<ElementId> {
675	        // Find and remove the entry for this scope.
676	        let idx = self.entries.iter().rposition(|e| e.scope_id == scope_id)?;
677	        let entry = self.entries.remove(idx);
678	
679	        // Out-of-order deactivation check: if this scope is NOT the top of
680	        // the stack, its saved target may reside inside a now-inactive outer
681	        // scope. In that case, skip restoration entirely — the outer scope
682	        // will handle it when it deactivates.
683	        if idx < self.entries.len() {
684	            // Inner scope deactivating after an outer scope was already removed.
685	            // The saved target is likely invalid. Return None to let the caller
686	            // fall through to the fallback chain.
687	            return None;
688	        }
689	
690	        // Fallback chain:
691	        // 1. Saved target, if it still exists in the DOM and is focusable.
692	        if let Some(ref el_id) = entry.saved_focus {
693	            if is_valid_restore_target(el_id) {
694	                return Some(el_id.clone());
695	            }
696	        }
697	        // 2. Trigger element, if valid.
698	        if let Some(ref trigger_id) = entry.trigger_element {
699	            if is_valid_restore_target(trigger_id) {
700	                return Some(trigger_id.clone());
701	            }
702	        }
703	        // 3. First tabbable element in the parent scope (caller handles).
704	        // 4. document.body (caller handles as final fallback).
705	        None
706	    }
707	}
708	
709	/// Validates that an element still exists in the DOM and is focusable.
710	fn is_valid_restore_target(el_id: &ElementId) -> bool {
711	    let Some(el) = document().get_element_by_id(el_id) else {
712	        return false;
713	    };
714	    // Element must be connected to the DOM and not hidden/disabled.
715	    el.is_connected() && is_focusable(&el)
716	}
717	```
718	
719	**SSR safety:** The `FocusRestorationStack` is a client-only construct. Adapters MUST gate its initialization:
720	
721	- **Leptos:** Wrap in `#[cfg(not(feature = "ssr"))]` or guard with `leptos::is_server()` check
722	- **Dioxus:** Initialize inside `use_effect` (which only runs on the client)
723	
724	If the stack is accidentally created on the server (e.g., via a global `thread_local!`), it could retain stale state across SSR requests in multi-tenant server environments.
725	
726	**Nested restoration priority:** When both an inner and outer scope deactivate (e.g., a
727	confirmation popover closes followed by its parent dialog), the inner scope restores first,
728	then the outer scope restores — each popping from the stack in LIFO order.
729	
730	**`saved_focus` validation:** Before restoring focus to a saved element, the adapter MUST
731	verify that the element (a) still exists in the DOM via `is_connected()` and (b) is
732	focusable (not `disabled`, not `display:none`, has valid `tabindex`). If validation fails,
733	the fallback chain proceeds.
734	
735	**Out-of-order deactivation:** If an inner scope deactivates after its outer scope has
736	already been removed (e.g., both close simultaneously but cleanup runs in arbitrary order),
737	the inner scope's saved target may point to an element inside the now-destroyed outer scope.
738	In this case, the stack skips the inner scope's restoration and defers to the outer scope's
739	fallback chain.
740	
741	**Fallback chain** (in priority order):
742	
743	1. The saved focus target, if still valid in the DOM and focusable
744	2. The scope's trigger element (the element that caused the scope to activate)
745	3. The first tabbable element in the parent scope
746	4. `document.body` as the final fallback
747	
748	## 9. Platform Notes
749	
750	> **Dioxus focus operations:** Focus operations (`focus_first`, `focus_last`,
751	> `restore_focus_safely`) use `PlatformEffects` trait methods (see `01-architecture.md`
752	> section 2.2.7). For Dioxus Desktop/Mobile, the adapter provides a platform implementation
753	> that routes these through native focus APIs for cross-platform compatibility.
754	>
755	> **Cleanup timing:** Leptos uses `on_cleanup` and Dioxus uses `use_drop` for teardown.
756	> In HMR/hot-reload scenarios, timing may differ — ensure the `FocusRestorationStack`
757	> is cleared on both cleanup and re-mount.
758	
759	## 10. Library Parity
760	
761	> Compared against: React Aria (`FocusScope`).
762	
763	### 10.1 Props
764	
765	| Feature       | ars-ui                | React Aria     | Notes                                    |
766	| ------------- | --------------------- | -------------- | ---------------------------------------- |
767	| Auto-focus    | `auto_focus`          | `autoFocus`    | Both libraries                           |
768	| Contain/trap  | `trapped` / `contain` | `contain`      | Both libraries; ars-ui offers both names |
769	| Restore focus | `restore_focus`       | `restoreFocus` | Both libraries                           |
770	
771	**Gaps:** None.
772	
773	### 10.2 Anatomy
774	
775	| Part      | ars-ui      | React Aria    | Notes                                  |
776	| --------- | ----------- | ------------- | -------------------------------------- |
777	| Container | `Container` | (wrapper div) | Both libraries use a container element |
778	
779	**Gaps:** None.
780	
781	### 10.3 Features
782	
783	| Feature                  | ars-ui                              | React Aria              |
784	| ------------------------ | ----------------------------------- | ----------------------- |
785	| Focus trapping           | Yes                                 | Yes                     |
786	| Focus restoration        | Yes                                 | Yes                     |
787	| Auto-focus first element | Yes                                 | Yes                     |
788	| FocusManager context     | Yes (`focus_next`/`focus_previous`) | Yes (`useFocusManager`) |
789	| Nested scope stack       | Yes (`FocusRestorationStack`)       | Yes (internal)          |
790	| Focus navigation options | Yes (`FocusNavigationOptions`)      | Yes (`wrap`, etc.)      |
791	
792	**Gaps:** None.
793	
794	### 10.4 Summary
795	
796	- **Overall:** Full parity.
797	- **Divergences:** ars-ui exposes both `trapped` and `contain` prop aliases. ars-ui explicitly defines `FocusRestorationStack` for nested scopes; React Aria handles this internally.
798	- **Recommended additions:** None.
799

1	//! `FocusScope` component state machine and connect API.
2	//!
3	//! `FocusScope` constrains keyboard Tab focus within a container, enabling
4	//! focus trapping for `Dialog`, `Drawer`, `Popover`, and other overlay
5	//! components. The agnostic core emits typed effect intents
6	//! ([`Effect`]) that adapters route through their `PlatformEffects`
7	//! implementation — the core never touches the DOM or framework helpers.
8	//!
9	//! See `spec/components/utility/focus-scope.md` for the full contract.
10	
11	use alloc::{rc::Rc, string::String};
12	use core::fmt::{self, Debug};
13	
14	use ars_core::{
15	    AttrMap, ComponentMessages, ComponentPart, ConnectApi, Env, HasId, HtmlAttr, PendingEffect,
16	    TransitionPlan,
17	};
18	
19	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
20	// State
21	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
22	
23	/// The states of the `FocusScope` component.
24	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
25	pub enum State {
26	    /// Focus scope is idle; Tab behavior is unmodified.
27	    Inactive,
28	
29	    /// Focus scope is active; the adapter is responsible for trapping Tab
30	    /// inside the container when `trapped` is true.
31	    Active {
32	        /// When true, Tab cannot escape the container.
33	        trapped: bool,
34	    },
35	}
36	
37	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
38	// Event
39	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
40	
41	/// The events of the `FocusScope` component.
42	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
43	pub enum Event {
44	    /// Activate the focus scope, optionally trapping focus.
45	    ///
46	    /// The adapter captures the currently focused element ID before sending
47	    /// this event (via `PlatformEffects::active_element_id`) so the machine
48	    /// can store it for later restoration.
49	    Activate {
50	        /// When true, Tab cannot escape the container.
51	        trapped: bool,
52	        /// ID of the element that had focus before activation (for
53	        /// restore-on-deactivate). Captured by the adapter via
54	        /// `PlatformEffects::active_element_id`.
55	        saved_focus_id: Option<String>,
56	    },
57	
58	    /// Deactivate the scope and optionally restore previous focus.
59	    Deactivate {
60	        /// When true, the machine emits [`Effect::RestoreFocus`] so the
61	        /// adapter can return focus to the previously focused element.
62	        restore_focus: bool,
63	    },
64	
65	    /// Enable focus trapping on an active scope (toggles `trapped` to true).
66	    TrapFocus,
67	
68	    /// Disable focus trapping on an active scope (toggles `trapped` to false).
69	    ReleaseTrap,
70	
71	    /// Request focus restoration to [`Context::saved_focus`].
72	    ///
73	    /// When `Inactive`, the machine emits [`Effect::RestoreFocus`]; when
74	    /// `Active`, the event is ignored — restoration is only meaningful
75	    /// after the scope has deactivated. Adapters MAY send this event
76	    /// explicitly when an `Inactive` scope still holds a saved target
77	    /// (e.g., nested-scope cleanup).
78	    RestoreFocus,
79	
80	    /// Request focus on the first tabbable element inside the container.
81	    /// Emits [`Effect::FocusFirst`] when `Active`; ignored otherwise.
82	    FocusFirst,
83	
84	    /// Request focus on the last tabbable element inside the container.
85	    /// Emits [`Effect::FocusLast`] when `Active`; ignored otherwise.
86	    FocusLast,
87	}
88	
89	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
90	// Context
91	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
92	
93	/// Machine context for the `FocusScope` component.
94	///
95	/// `is_active()` and `is_trapped()` are derived from [`State`] in the
96	/// connect API — they are not stored here.
97	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]
98	pub struct Context {
99	    /// The element that had focus before the scope was activated.
100	    ///
101	    /// Set on [`Event::Activate`] and read by the adapter when dispatching
102	    /// [`Effect::RestoreFocus`]. The agnostic core does not clear the value
103	    /// after restoration — the next `Activate` overwrites it.
104	    pub saved_focus: Option<String>,
105	
106	    /// The DOM ID of the container element that scopes focus.
107	    ///
108	    /// Adapters populate this via `Service::context_mut()` once they have
109	    /// resolved the container's stable ID. The agnostic core treats it as
110	    /// an opaque token.
111	    pub container_id: Option<String>,
112	}
113	
114	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
115	// Props
116	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
117	
118	/// Props for the `FocusScope` component.
119	#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, HasId)]
120	pub struct Props {
121	    /// Component instance ID. Adapters set this from a hydration-safe
122	    /// stable ID generator.
123	    pub id: String,
124	
125	    /// Prevent Tab from moving focus outside the container.
126	    pub trapped: bool,
127	
128	    /// Alias for [`trapped`](Self::trapped) — clearer naming in some
129	    /// contexts (e.g., when `trapped` already means "active" elsewhere).
130	    pub contain: bool,
131	
132	    /// On activation, automatically move focus to the first tabbable
133	    /// element (or the element with `autofocus` attribute, if any).
134	    pub auto_focus: bool,
135	
136	    /// On deactivation, restore focus to the previously focused element.
137	    pub restore_focus: bool,
138	}
139	
140	impl Default for Props {
141	    fn default() -> Self {
142	        Self {
143	            id: String::new(),
144	            trapped: false,
145	            contain: false,
146	            auto_focus: true,
147	            restore_focus: true,
148	        }
149	    }
150	}
151	
152	impl Props {
153	    /// Returns a fresh [`Props`] with every field at its [`Default`] value.
154	    #[must_use]
155	    pub fn new() -> Self {
156	        Self::default()
157	    }
158	
159	    /// Sets [`id`](Self::id).
160	    #[must_use]
161	    pub fn id(mut self, id: impl Into<String>) -> Self {
162	        self.id = id.into();
163	        self
164	    }
165	
166	    /// Sets [`trapped`](Self::trapped).
167	    #[must_use]
168	    pub const fn trapped(mut self, trapped: bool) -> Self {
169	        self.trapped = trapped;
170	        self
171	    }
172	
173	    /// Sets [`contain`](Self::contain).
174	    #[must_use]
175	    pub const fn contain(mut self, contain: bool) -> Self {
176	        self.contain = contain;
177	        self
178	    }
179	
180	    /// Sets [`auto_focus`](Self::auto_focus).
181	    #[must_use]
182	    pub const fn auto_focus(mut self, auto_focus: bool) -> Self {
183	        self.auto_focus = auto_focus;
184	        self
185	    }
186	
187	    /// Sets [`restore_focus`](Self::restore_focus).
188	    #[must_use]
189	    pub const fn restore_focus(mut self, restore_focus: bool) -> Self {
190	        self.restore_focus = restore_focus;
191	        self
192	    }
193	}
194	
195	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
196	// Messages
197	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
198	
199	/// `FocusScope` has no translatable strings.
200	#[derive(Clone, Debug, Default, PartialEq, Eq)]
201	pub struct Messages;
202	
203	impl ComponentMessages for Messages {}
204	
205	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
206	// Effect
207	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
208	
209	/// Typed effect intents emitted by the `FocusScope` machine.
210	///
211	/// Each variant is an adapter contract — the adapter's effect handler
212	/// translates the variant into the corresponding `PlatformEffects` call.
213	/// See `spec/components/utility/focus-scope.md` §1.8 for the per-variant
214	/// contract.
215	#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
216	pub enum Effect {
217	    /// Install the Tab-key interception (keydown handler + sentinel
218	    /// elements) that keeps focus inside the scope while it is active.
219	    ///
220	    /// Cleanup tears the handler back down — invoked when the machine
221	    /// emits `.cancel_effect(Effect::FocusTrapListener)` on `Deactivate`.
222	    FocusTrapListener,
223	
224	    /// Move focus to the first tabbable descendant of the container.
225	    /// Fall back to focusing the container itself when none exist.
226	    FocusFirst,
227	
228	    /// Move focus to the last tabbable descendant of the container.
229	    /// Fall back to focusing the container itself when none exist.
230	    FocusLast,
231	
232	    /// Restore focus to `service.context().saved_focus`, applying the
233	    /// §7 fallback chain when the saved target is no longer focusable.
234	    RestoreFocus,
235	}
236	
237	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
238	// Machine
239	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
240	
241	/// State machine for the `FocusScope` component.
242	#[derive(Debug)]
243	pub struct Machine;
244	
245	impl ars_core::Machine for Machine {
246	    type State = State;
247	    type Event = Event;
248	    type Context = Context;
249	    type Props = Props;
250	    type Messages = Messages;
251	    type Effect = Effect;
252	    type Api<'a> = Api<'a>;
253	
254	    fn init(
255	        _props: &Self::Props,
256	        _env: &Env,
257	        _messages: &Self::Messages,
258	    ) -> (Self::State, Self::Context) {
259	        (State::Inactive, Context::default())
260	    }
261	
262	    fn transition(
263	        state: &Self::State,
264	        event: &Self::Event,
265	        _ctx: &Self::Context,
266	        props: &Self::Props,
267	    ) -> Option<TransitionPlan<Self>> {
268	        match (state, event) {
269	            // ── Activation ──────────────────────────────────────────────
270	            (
271	                State::Inactive,
272	                Event::Activate {
273	                    trapped,
274	                    saved_focus_id,
275	                },
276	            ) => {
277	                let trap = *trapped || props.contain;
278	                let auto_focus = props.auto_focus;
279	                let saved = saved_focus_id.clone();
280	                let mut plan = TransitionPlan::to(State::Active { trapped: trap })
281	                    .apply(move |ctx: &mut Context| {
282	                        ctx.saved_focus = saved;
283	                    })
284	                    .with_effect(PendingEffect::named(Effect::FocusTrapListener));
285	                if auto_focus {
286	                    plan = plan.then(Event::FocusFirst);
287	                }
288	                Some(plan)
289	            }
290	
291	            // ── Deactivation ────────────────────────────────────────────
292	            (State::Active { .. }, Event::Deactivate { restore_focus }) => {
293	                let restore = *restore_focus;
294	                let mut plan =
295	                    TransitionPlan::to(State::Inactive).cancel_effect(Effect::FocusTrapListener);
296	                if restore {
297	                    plan = plan.with_effect(PendingEffect::named(Effect::RestoreFocus));
298	                } else {
299	                    plan = plan.apply(|ctx: &mut Context| {
300	                        ctx.saved_focus = None;
301	                    });
302	                }
303	                Some(plan)
304	            }
305	
306	            // ── Trap / Release ──────────────────────────────────────────
307	            (State::Active { trapped: false }, Event::TrapFocus) => {
308	                Some(TransitionPlan::to(State::Active { trapped: true }))
309	            }
310	            (State::Active { trapped: true }, Event::ReleaseTrap) => {
311	                Some(TransitionPlan::to(State::Active { trapped: false }))
312	            }
313	
314	            // ── RestoreFocus ────────────────────────────────────────────
315	            // While `Active`, `RestoreFocus` is ignored by the wildcard
316	            // arm at the bottom — restoration is only meaningful after
317	            // the scope has deactivated.
318	            (State::Inactive, Event::RestoreFocus) => {
319	                Some(TransitionPlan::new().with_effect(PendingEffect::named(Effect::RestoreFocus)))
320	            }
321	
322	            // ── Focus Navigation ────────────────────────────────────────
323	            (State::Active { .. }, Event::FocusFirst) => {
324	                Some(TransitionPlan::new().with_effect(PendingEffect::named(Effect::FocusFirst)))
325	            }
326	            (State::Active { .. }, Event::FocusLast) => {
327	                Some(TransitionPlan::new().with_effect(PendingEffect::named(Effect::FocusLast)))
328	            }
329	
330	            _ => None,
331	        }
332	    }
333	
334	    fn connect<'a>(
335	        state: &'a Self::State,
336	        ctx: &'a Self::Context,
337	        props: &'a Self::Props,
338	        send: &'a dyn Fn(Self::Event),
339	    ) -> Self::Api<'a> {
340	        Api {
341	            state,
342	            ctx,
343	            props,
344	            send,
345	        }
346	    }
347	}
348	
349	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
350	// Part
351	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
352	
353	/// Anatomy parts of the `FocusScope` component.
354	#[derive(ars_core::ComponentPart)]
355	#[scope = "focus-scope"]
356	pub enum Part {
357	    /// The container element that scopes focus.
358	    Container,
359	}
360	
361	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
362	// Api
363	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
364	
365	/// Connect API for the `FocusScope` component.
366	pub struct Api<'a> {
367	    /// The current state of the focus scope.
368	    state: &'a State,
369	    /// The context of the focus scope.
370	    ctx: &'a Context,
371	    /// The props of the focus scope.
372	    props: &'a Props,
373	    /// The send function for the focus scope.
374	    send: &'a dyn Fn(Event),
375	}
376	
377	impl Debug for Api<'_> {
378	    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {
379	        f.debug_struct("focus_scope::Api")
380	            .field("state", self.state)
381	            .field("ctx", self.ctx)
382	            .field("props", self.props)
383	            .finish_non_exhaustive()
384	    }
385	}
386	
387	impl Api<'_> {
388	    /// Returns the current [`State`] of the focus scope.
389	    #[must_use]
390	    pub const fn state(&self) -> &State {
391	        self.state
392	    }
393	
394	    /// Returns the current [`Context`] of the focus scope.
395	    #[must_use]
396	    pub const fn context(&self) -> &Context {
397	        self.ctx
398	    }
399	
400	    /// Returns the [`Props`] used by the focus scope.
401	    #[must_use]
402	    pub const fn props(&self) -> &Props {
403	        self.props
404	    }
405	
406	    /// Whether the focus scope is currently active.
407	    #[must_use]
408	    pub const fn is_active(&self) -> bool {
409	        matches!(self.state, State::Active { .. })
410	    }
411	
412	    /// Whether the focus scope is currently trapping focus.
413	    #[must_use]
414	    pub const fn is_trapped(&self) -> bool {
415	        matches!(self.state, State::Active { trapped: true })
416	    }
417	
418	    /// Attributes for the container element that scopes focus.
419	    ///
420	    /// Always emits `data-ars-scope="focus-scope"` and
421	    /// `data-ars-part="container"`. When active, adds `data-ars-active`
422	    /// and `tabindex="-1"` so the container can be a programmatic focus
423	    /// target when no tabbable children exist yet. When trapped, adds
424	    /// `data-ars-trapped`.
425	    #[must_use]
426	    pub fn container_attrs(&self) -> AttrMap {
427	        let mut attrs = AttrMap::new();
428	        let [(scope_attr, scope_val), (part_attr, part_val)] = Part::Container.data_attrs();
429	        attrs.set(scope_attr, scope_val).set(part_attr, part_val);
430	        if self.is_active() {
431	            attrs
432	                .set_bool(HtmlAttr::Data("ars-active"), true)
433	                .set(HtmlAttr::TabIndex, "-1");
434	        }
435	        if self.is_trapped() {
436	            attrs.set_bool(HtmlAttr::Data("ars-trapped"), true);
437	        }
438	        attrs
439	    }
440	
441	    /// Imperatively activate the focus scope.
442	    ///
443	    /// `saved_focus_id` is the ID of the currently focused element,
444	    /// captured by the adapter via `PlatformEffects::active_element_id`
445	    /// before calling this.
446	    pub fn activate(&self, trapped: bool, saved_focus_id: Option<String>) {
447	        (self.send)(Event::Activate {
448	            trapped,
449	            saved_focus_id,
450	        });
451	    }
452	
453	    /// Imperatively deactivate the focus scope.
454	    pub fn deactivate(&self, restore_focus: bool) {
455	        (self.send)(Event::Deactivate { restore_focus });
456	    }
457	
458	    /// Request that focus move to the first tabbable descendant of the
459	    /// container. Sends [`Event::FocusFirst`].
460	    pub fn focus_first(&self) {
461	        (self.send)(Event::FocusFirst);
462	    }
463	
464	    /// Request that focus move to the last tabbable descendant of the
465	    /// container. Sends [`Event::FocusLast`].
466	    pub fn focus_last(&self) {
467	        (self.send)(Event::FocusLast);
468	    }
469	
470	    /// Move focus to the next focusable element within the scope.
471	    ///
472	    /// Returns `true` if focus was moved, `false` otherwise. The agnostic
473	    /// core cannot resolve tabbable elements synchronously and returns
474	    /// `false` as its stable contract — adapters layer a platform-aware
475	    /// variant on top (used by `Toolbar`, `ActionGroup`, `TreeView`).
476	    #[must_use]
477	    pub const fn focus_next(&self, _opts: &FocusNavigationOptions) -> bool {
478	        false
479	    }
480	
481	    /// Move focus to the previous focusable element within the scope.
482	    ///
483	    /// Returns `true` if focus was moved, `false` otherwise. Adapter-
484	    /// overridable; see [`focus_next`](Self::focus_next).
485	    #[must_use]
486	    pub const fn focus_previous(&self, _opts: &FocusNavigationOptions) -> bool {
487	        false
488	    }
489	}
490	
491	impl ConnectApi for Api<'_> {
492	    type Part = Part;
493	
494	    fn part_attrs(&self, part: Part) -> AttrMap {
495	        match part {
496	            Part::Container => self.container_attrs(),
497	        }
498	    }
499	}
500	
501	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
502	// FocusNavigationOptions
503	// ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
504	
505	/// Reference-counted predicate filter used by [`FocusNavigationOptions::accept`].
506	///
507	/// Wrapping the trait object in [`Rc`] keeps [`FocusNavigationOptions`]
508	/// trivially [`Clone`] while letting consumers share the predicate
509	/// across multiple calls without re-allocating.
510	pub type AcceptFn = Rc<dyn Fn(&str) -> bool>;
511	
512	/// Options for adapter-provided focus navigation.
513	///
514	/// The agnostic core only carries the option payload; behavior lives in
515	/// each adapter's platform-aware override of [`Api::focus_next`] and
516	/// [`Api::focus_previous`].
517	#[derive(Clone)]
518	pub struct FocusNavigationOptions {
519	    /// Wrap around at boundaries.
520	    pub wrap: bool,
521	    /// Element ID to start from (default: currently focused element).
522	    pub from: Option<String>,
523	    /// Only consider tabbable elements (`tabindex >= 0`).
524	    pub tabbable: bool,
525	    /// Custom filter predicate that receives an element ID.
526	    pub accept: Option<AcceptFn>,
527	}
528	
529	impl Debug for FocusNavigationOptions {
530	    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {
531	        f.debug_struct("FocusNavigationOptions")
532	            .field("wrap", &self.wrap)
533	            .field("from", &self.from)
534	            .field("tabbable", &self.tabbable)
535	            .field("accept", &self.accept.as_ref().map(|_| "<predicate>"))
536	            .finish()
537	    }
538	}

1113	    // ── Api imperative methods send the expected events ─────────────
1114	
1115	    use core::cell::RefCell;
1116	
1117	    fn capture<F: FnOnce(&dyn Fn(Event))>(invoke: F) -> Vec<Event> {
1118	        let captured: RefCell<Vec<Event>> = RefCell::new(Vec::new());
1119	        let sender = |event: Event| captured.borrow_mut().push(event);
1120	        invoke(&sender);
1121	        captured.into_inner()
1122	    }
1123	
1124	    fn api_with<'a>(
1125	        state: &'a State,
1126	        ctx: &'a Context,
1127	        props: &'a Props,
1128	        send: &'a dyn Fn(Event),
1129	    ) -> Api<'a> {
1130	        Api {
1131	            state,
1132	            ctx,
1133	            props,
1134	            send,
1135	        }
1136	    }
1137

1140	        let state = State::Inactive;

1145	            let api = api_with(&state, &ctx, &props, sender);
1146	            api.activate(true, Some("trigger".into()));
1147	        });
1148	
1149	        assert_eq!(
1150	            events,
1151	            vec![Event::Activate {
1152	                trapped: true,
1153	                saved_focus_id: Some("trigger".into()),
1154	            }]
1155	        );
1156	    }

content.text:
## Phase 1 — Drift Findings

| # | Severity | Spec says | Impl does | Best outcome |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| F1 | Low | `Context` derives `Clone, Debug, PartialEq` | Adds `Eq, Default` | Spec catches up — `Eq, Default` are workspace conventions and let the machine call `Context::default()` |
| F2 | Low | `Messages` derives `Clone, Debug, Default, PartialEq` | Adds `Eq` | Spec catches up — matches `live_region::Messages` |
| F3 | Medium | `Props` has no builder methods | Impl has `.id() / .trapped() / .contain() / .auto_focus() / .restore_focus()` | Spec catches up — matches `live_region::Props` and is widely used |
| F4 | Low | `is_active` / `is_trapped` are `pub fn -> bool` | `pub const fn` | Spec catches up — cheap to const |
| F5 | Medium | `focus_next(opts: FocusNavigationOptions) -> bool` (by value) | `focus_next(_opts: &FocusNavigationOptions) -> bool` (by reference) | Spec catches up — by-reference is idiomatic for shared option bundles |
| F6 | Medium | No `Api::state() / context() / props()` accessors | All three exist as `pub const fn` | Spec catches up — useful for adapter tests and consumer introspection |
| F7 | Low | No `FocusNavigationOptions::default()` impl | Has it | Spec catches up — needed for ergonomic construction |
| F8 | Low | No `AcceptFn` type alias | Has `pub type AcceptFn = Rc<dyn Fn(&str) -> bool>` | Spec catches up — needed for clippy `type_complexity` and improves docstrings |
| F9 | Low | No `Debug` impl for `Api` or `FocusNavigationOptions` | Has both | Spec catches up — workspace `missing-debug-implementations` lint requires this |
| F10 | Low | §1.6 imports `Machine as _, Messages as _` | Imports `ComponentMessages` (real trait name) | Spec catches up — `Messages as _` clashes with the local `Messages` struct in the file |
| F11 | Low | §1.7 `container_attrs` is plain `pub fn` | `#[must_use] pub fn` | Spec catches up — `#[must_use]` reflects that callers compose the result |

12 spec-only updates. Landing them now.

# FocusScope — Leptos Adapter

## 1. Purpose and Adapter Scope

This spec maps the core [`FocusScope`](../../components/utility/focus-scope.md) machine to Leptos 0.8.x.

## 2. Public Adapter API

```rust,no_check
#[component] pub fn FocusScope(...) -> impl IntoView
```

The adapter exposes the full core focus-scope prop set for trapping, looping, autofocus, and restore-focus behavior.

## 3. Mapping to Core Component Contract

- Props parity: full parity.
- Event parity: focus containment and restoration are adapter-driven on top of the core machine.

## 4. Part Mapping

| Core part / structure      | Required?                | Adapter rendering target                   | Ownership      | Attr source                                    | Notes                                        |
| -------------------------- | ------------------------ | ------------------------------------------ | -------------- | ---------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------- |
| `Container`                | required                 | wrapper `<div>` or any chosen root element | adapter-owned  | `api.part_attrs(focus_scope::Part::Container)` | Focus trap boundary.                         |
| trapped descendants region | required structural node | consumer children inside container         | consumer-owned | none                                           | Focus stays within this region while active. |

## 5. Attr Merge and Ownership Rules

| Target node           | Core attrs                                 | Adapter-owned attrs                                     | Consumer attrs      | Merge order                                                     | Ownership notes                                         |
| --------------------- | ------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- |
| `Container`           | `api.part_attrs(Part::Container)`          | focus-sentinel or guard attrs if the strategy uses them | consumer root attrs | core focus-boundary attrs win; `class`/`style` merge additively | adapter-owned scope container                           |
| helper nodes (if any) | none from core unless modeled as structure | sentinel, guard, or helper attrs                        | none                | adapter attrs apply directly                                    | helper nodes are adapter-owned and never consumer-owned |

## 6. Composition / Context Contract

May compose with `ArsProvider` for scoped DOM queries and with `Dismissable` inside the same container.
When an adapter chooses to expose programmatic navigation, it should publish a `FocusManager`-style context with `focus_next`, `focus_previous`, `focus_first`, and `focus_last` helpers for descendants that need imperative focus movement without bypassing the scope contract.

## 7. Prop Sync and Event Mapping

Focus-scope behavior is mostly interaction-driven. Configuration props are usually init-only unless a wrapper explicitly reinitializes the scope.

## 2. Public Adapter API

```

## 3. Mapping to Core Component Contract

- Props parity: full parity.
- Event parity: focus containment and restoration are adapter-driven on top of the core machine.

## 4. Part Mapping

| Core part / structure      | Required?                | Adapter rendering target                   | Ownership      | Attr source                                    | Notes                                        |
| -------------------------- | ------------------------ | ------------------------------------------ | -------------- | ---------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------- |
| `Container`                | required                 | wrapper `<div>` or any chosen root element | adapter-owned  | `api.part_attrs(focus_scope::Part::Container)` | Focus trap boundary.                         |
| trapped descendants region | required structural node | consumer children inside container         | consumer-owned | none                                           | Focus stays within this region while active. |

## 5. Attr Merge and Ownership Rules

| Target node           | Core attrs                                 | Adapter-owned attrs                                     | Consumer attrs      | Merge order                                                     | Ownership notes                                         |
| --------------------- | ------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- |
| `Container`           | `api.part_attrs(Part::Container)`          | focus-sentinel or guard attrs if the strategy uses them | consumer root attrs | core focus-boundary attrs win; `class`/`style` merge additively | adapter-owned scope container                           |
| helper nodes (if any) | none from core unless modeled as structure | sentinel, guard, or helper attrs                        | none                | adapter attrs apply directly                                    | helper nodes are adapter-owned and never consumer-owned |

## 7. Prop Sync and Event Mapping

Focus-scope behavior is mostly interaction-driven. Configuration props are usually init-only unless a wrapper explicitly reinitializes the scope.

Recommended Implementation Sequence 129 130 1. Establish the scope container ref and any helper-node strategy. 131 2. Activate the scope on mount using the documented machine rules. 132 3. Publish optional `FocusManager` context after the scope boundary and activation path are stable. 133 4. Wire Tab/Shift+Tab trapping and nested-scope stack behavior. 134 5. Add restore-focus cleanup and fallback behavior. 135 6. Verify client-only activation, optional context publication, and cleanup test oracles. 136 137 ## 18. Anti-Patterns 138 139 - Do not activate focus trapping during SSR. 140 - Do not store restore targets as IDs only when the contract requires a live node handle. 141 - Do not let nested scopes unwind out of stack order. 142 143 ## 19. Consumer Expectations and Guarantees 144 145 - Consumers may assume focus trapping and restore-focus ordering follow the active scope stack. 146 - Consumers may assume optional focus-manager publication, when exposed, already respects the current scope boundary. 147 - Consumers must not assume focus logic runs during SSR or before the container ref exists. 148 149 ## 20. Platform Support Matrix 150 151 | Capability / behavior | Browser client | SSR | Notes | 152 | ---------------------------------- | -------------- | -------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------- | 153 | focus trapping and restore | full support | client-only | SSR renders structure only. | 154 | optional focus-manager publication | full support | SSR-safe empty | Context may publish without live focus side effects until mount. | 155 156 ## 21. Debug Diagnostics and Production Policy 157 158 | Condition | Debug build behavior | Production behavior | Notes | 159 | ------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------- | ------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- | 160 | documented platform capability is unavailable on the active runtime | debug warning | degrade gracefully | Use the documented fallback path instead of inventing browser-only behavior. | 161 162 ## 22. Shared Adapter Helper Notes 163 164 | Helper concept | Required? | Responsibility | Reused by | Notes | 165 | --------------------------------- | ----------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- | 166 | focus-manager publication helper | recommended | Publish `focus_next`, `focus_previous`, `focus_first`, and `focus_last` without leaking raw focus internals. | `focus-scope`, composed overlay utilities | Only needed when the adapter exposes programmatic navigation. | 167 | node-boundary registration helper | required | Track the active scope container, helper nodes, and restore target handles. | `focus-scope`, `dismissable` | Focus restoration depends on live-node ownership. | 168 169 ## 23. Framework-Specific Behavior 170 171 Leptos uses `on_cleanup` for focus restoration cleanup. When programmatic navigation is exposed, the adapter should publish a `FocusManager` through `provide_context(FocusManager { ... })` after the container ref and scope activation path exist. 172 173 ## 24. Canonical Implementation Sketch 174 175 ```rust 176 #[component] 177 pub fn FocusScope(children: Children) -> impl IntoView { 178 let machine = use_machine::(focus_scope::Props::default()); 179 let attrs = machine.derive(|api| api.part_attrs(focus_scope::Part::Container)); 180 view! {
{children()}
} 181 } 182 ``` 183 184 ## 25. Reference Implementation Skeleton 185 186 ```rust,no_check 187 let machine = use_machine::(props); 188 let container_ref = create_container_ref(); 189 let scope_stack = create_scope_registration_helper(); 190 let restore_target = store_previous_focus_handle(); 191 let focus_manager = maybe_publish_focus_manager(machine, container_ref); 192 193 render_scope_container_and_optional_helper_nodes(container_ref); 194 activate_scope_on_mount(scope_stack, container_ref); 195 wire_tab_looping_and_nested_scope_order(machine, scope_stack); 196 197 on_cleanup(|| { 198 scope_stack.unregister(); 199 restore_focus_if_needed(restore_target); 200 release_helper_nodes(); 201 }); 202 ``` 203 204 ## 26. Adapter Invariants 205 206 - All trapped descendants must remain inside the owned scope container used as the focus boundary. 207 - Nested scope activation and restore-focus ordering must remain deterministic. 208 - Restore-focus fallback behavior must be explicit when the previously focused element no longer exists. 209 - Focus sentinels, guards, or helper dismiss controls must remain inside the trapped structure whenever they are rendered. 210 - Client-only focus trapping logic must not execute during SSR. 211 212 ## 27. Accessibility and SSR Notes 213 214 Trap behavior is client-only, but the container structure must still be documented and rendered consistently. 215 216 ## 28. Parity Summary and Intentional Deviations 217 218 Parity summary: full core prop, part, and lifecycle parity. 219 220 Intentional deviations: none. 221 222 Traceability note: This adapter spec now makes explicit the core adapter-owned concerns for live restore-target validation, nested scope ordering, client-only trap activation, helper-node ownership, and optional focus-manager publication. 223 224 ## 29. Test Scenarios 225 226 - container mapping 227 - trap activation 228 - nested scope behavior 229 - focus restore fallback 230 - descendants can access focus navigation helpers through context when the adapter exposes them 231 232 ## 30. Test Oracle Notes 233 234 | Behavior | Preferred oracle type | Notes | 235 | ---------------------------------- | --------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | 236 | nested scope stack behavior | machine state | Assert active-scope stack order and restoration policy. | 237 | focus restoration | cleanup side effects | Verify focus lands on the documented restore target or fallback after cleanup. | 238 | scope container/helper structure | rendered structure | Assert the container and any helper nodes remain correctly placed. | 239 | optional focus-manager publication | context registration | Verify descendants can read the published focus navigation helpers when the adapter exposes them. | 240 241 Cheap verification recipe: 242 243 1. Render a scope with a focusable child, activate trapping on the client, and assert the container plus helper structure before moving focus. 244 2. Open nested scopes, then close them in reverse order and verify focus restores to the documented live target or fallback. 245 3. If the adapter publishes `FocusManager`, read it from a descendant and verify the context handle exists without using it as proof of trap activation by itself. 246 247 ## 31. Implementation Checklist 248 249 - [ ] Container ref and any helper-node refs exist before trap activation. 250 - [ ] Nested scope activation and restore order match the documented rules. 251 - [ ] Restore-focus fallback behavior is verified. 252 - [ ] Optional `FocusManager` publication is documented and verified when the adapter exposes programmatic navigation. 253 - [ ] Cleanup and test oracles cover focus restoration and structure. 254

Registration and Cleanup Contract - Each active focus scope registers in a scope stack on mount. - Nested scopes must unwind in last-in, first-out order. - Helper nodes or sentinels, if used, are owned by the scope instance and cleaned up before focus restoration completes. | Registered entity | Registration trigger | Identity key | Cleanup trigger | Cleanup action | Notes | | ------------------------ | --------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------- | --------------- | ---------------------------------------------- | ------------------- | | active scope stack entry | client-side mount with scope activation | scope instance | scope cleanup | remove stack entry and run restore-focus logic | no SSR registration | | helper nodes / sentinels | helper-node render | scope instance plus helper identity | scope cleanup | remove helper nodes and drop refs | optional strategy | ## 9. Ref and Node Contract | Target part / node | Ref required? | Ref owner | Node availability | Composition rule | Notes | | ----------------------------------- | --------------------------------------- | ------------------------------ | -------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | `Container` | yes | adapter-owned | required after mount | compose only when wrappers expose the scope container | The trap boundary requires a concrete node. | | helper nodes or sentinels when used | yes | adapter-owned | client-only | no consumer composition unless the strategy explicitly exposes them | These handles are part of the trap implementation, not consumer-owned structure. | | restore-focus target | yes if a previously focused node exists | adapter-owned transient handle | client-only | not compositional | Stored as a transient node handle, not a rendered part. | ## 10. State Machine Boundary Rules - machine-owned state: active scope status, nesting behavior, trap/loop semantics, and restore-focus decision rules. - adapter-local derived bookkeeping: transient previous-focus handle, helper-node refs, and active-scope stack handles. - forbidden local mirrors: do not fork a parallel active/inactive scope flag separate from the machine/scope stack. - allowed snapshot-read contexts: mount activation effects, Tab handlers, restore-focus cleanup, and render derivation. ## 11. Callback Payload Contract | Callback | Payload source | Payload shape | Timing | Cancelable? | Notes | | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------- | ------------- | -------------- | ----------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | no public adapter-specific callback unless a wrapper exposes lifecycle hooks | none | none | not applicable | no | Focus restoration and trap behavior remain internal unless a wrapper explicitly surfaces them. | ## 12. Failure and Degradation Rules | Condition | Policy | Notes | | ---------------------------------------- | ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ | | scope container ref missing after mount | fail fast | Focus trapping requires the container node. | | previous focus target missing on restore | degrade gracefully | Fall back to the documented restore target behavior instead of erroring. | | browser focus APIs absent during SSR | no-op | Render the structure and defer focus logic to the client. | ## 13. SSR and Client Boundary Rules - SSR must render the scope container and trapped descendants region. - Scope activation, helper-node setup, and restore-focus logic are client-only. - Container/helper refs are server-safe absent and required after mount. - Nested-scope structural ownership must remain consistent across hydration. ## 15. Performance Constraints - Active-scope registration must only change on mount/unmount or activation changes, not every render. - Helper-node setup should be instance-scoped and must not churn when the trapped children rerender. - Restore-focus cleanup should run once per scope cleanup and should not scan the DOM repeatedly when a stored target exists. ## 16. Publish optional `FocusManager` context after the scope boundary and activation path are stable. 4. Wire Tab/Shift+Tab trapping and nested-scope stack behavior. 5. Add restore-focus cleanup and fallback behavior. 6. Verify client-only activation, optional context publication, and cleanup test oracles. ## 18. content.description: Edit spec/components/utility/focus-scope.md §1.6, §1.6.1, §1.7 to replace inline use_platform_effects() calls with PendingEffect::named(Effect::*) markers, add Effect enum, convert restore_focus_safely to narrative bullets, remove get_tabbable_elements from Api, add Effect Contract subsection.
